title: FBS Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026)

meta_description: FBS Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026). FBS (3.9★, Free). Step-by-step setup, features walkthrough, and pro tips. Updated 2026.
excerpt: Complete guide to setting up and using FBS — from account creation to pro-level tips.
content:
What is FBS?

FBS is a global retail forex and CFD broker founded in 2009 that specializes in making currency trading accessible to everyone, from absolute beginners to experienced traders. With a 3.9/5 rating on TradingToolsHub and account types that require as little as $1 to get started, FBS has built its reputation around ultra-low barriers to entry and flexible leverage options. The broker supports MetaTrader 4 and MetaTrader 5 platforms plus its proprietary FBS Trader app, giving traders multiple ways to execute strategies across 40+ currency pairs, metals, indices, stocks, crypto, and energies. FBS is best suited for traders with limited capital, those exploring copy trading, or anyone seeking high-leverage opportunities—though the platform also serves intermediate and advanced traders with advanced features like backtesting, alerts, and automated trading.

How to Create Your FBS Account

Creating an FBS account takes less than 10 minutes and requires minimal documentation for a demo account, though live trading requires identity verification. Here's the step-by-step process:

  • Go to the FBS registration page and click "Open Account" or "Sign Up." You'll need your email address and a password that meets FBS security requirements (typically 8+ characters with mixed case and numbers).
  • Choose your account type during signup. You can select Demo (free, for practice), Cent Account ($0 minimum, 0.01 lot size), Standard Account ($0 minimum, 0.1 lot size), Zero Spread Account, or ECN Account. Most beginners start with Demo to learn the platform risk-free.
  • Select your base currency (USD, EUR, GBP, etc.) and preferred trading platform (MT4, MT5, or FBS Trader app).
  • Verify your email by clicking the confirmation link FBS sends within minutes.
  • Complete identity verification to deposit real money. You'll upload a government-issued ID (passport, driver's license, or national ID) and proof of address (utility bill, bank statement, or government document dated within the last 90 days). This typically takes 1-3 hours, though it can be faster during business hours.
  • Add a payment method from FBS's supported options: credit/debit card, bank transfer, e-wallets (Skrill, Neteller, Perfect Money), or cryptocurrency depending on your region.
  • Make your first deposit. Since FBS accepts deposits as low as $1, you can start live trading with minimal risk even while learning.

The entire process from signup to first trade typically takes 15-30 minutes if your documents are clear and submitted during business hours. FBS's 24/7 multilingual support team can help if you get stuck at any stage.

Setting Up FBS for the First Time

Once you've logged into FBS, you'll see your account dashboard, which displays your account balance, equity, margin level, and open positions. Unlike a complex terminal, FBS keeps the main view clean with your account summary front and center.

Configure your account settings: Click the account settings menu (usually a gear icon) to set your leverage level. FBS allows up to 1:3000 leverage, but beginners should start conservatively at 1:100 or 1:200 to control risk. You can also set your base currency preference, enable two-factor authentication for security, and configure notification preferences (email or push alerts).

Download your trading platform: From the FBS dashboard, download MetaTrader 4 or MetaTrader 5 depending on your preference. MT5 is newer with more order types and timeframes, while MT4 remains the industry standard with more third-party indicators and EAs (Expert Advisors). Log in using your FBS account credentials, and your live account will be ready to trade immediately. You can also access the web-based versions without downloading.

Explore the FBS Trader app: FBS's proprietary mobile app offers a simplified trading interface optimized for phones. Install it from the App Store or Google Play, log in, and you'll have the same account with real-time alerts and one-touch trading. This app is particularly useful for traders monitoring trades throughout the day.

Enable paper trading: Before risking real capital, switch to FBS's paper trading mode or open a separate Demo account. This lets you test your strategy and familiarize yourself with the platform using virtual money with real market prices.

Essential Features You Should Know

1. Copy Trading: FBS's copy trading feature is available on all account types at no extra cost. In your account dashboard, find the "Copy Trading" section and browse successful traders' profiles. You can copy their trades automatically, adjusting the copy volume to match your account size. This is ideal for beginners who want to learn by following experienced traders' strategies in real-time.

2. MetaTrader 4/5 Integration: Both platforms sync directly with your FBS account, offering access to the vast ecosystem of custom indicators, expert advisors (EAs), and trading signals. MT4 remains popular for copy trading and simple automation, while MT5 provides more advanced order types (pending orders, hedging) and 21 timeframes instead of MT4's 9.

3. Backtesting: Both MT4 and MT5 include built-in backtesting tools. You can test your strategies against historical price data from FBS without risking real money. Open the Strategy Tester in MT4/MT5, load your EA or manually test a strategy, and run it against 1-5 years of price history. Results show profit/loss, win rate, drawdown, and other metrics to validate your approach before going live.

4. Alerts and Notifications: Set price alerts, technical indicator alerts, and economic calendar alerts directly in MT4/MT5 or through the FBS Trader app. For example, alert when EUR/USD breaks above 1.1000, when RSI crosses above 70, or when major economic data is released. You'll receive push notifications, SMS (on some account types), or email alerts depending on your settings.

5. Mobile Trading via FBS Trader App: The proprietary FBS Trader app simplifies mobile trading with a cleaner interface than MT4/MT5 mobile versions. You can monitor positions, check account balance, view live quotes, set alerts, and execute trades from anywhere. The app also integrates FBS's educational materials and news feed, making it a one-stop platform for active traders.

6. Automated Trading (EAs and Scripts): Both MetaTrader platforms support Expert Advisors—automated programs that trade according to your rules. You can write your own in MQL4/MQL5, download free or paid EAs from the MQL Marketplace, or hire developers to code strategies. FBS supports unlimited EAs per account, so you can run multiple automated strategies simultaneously.

7. Performance Analytics and Risk Management: FBS provides real-time statistics on your trading: win rate, average trade duration, profit factor, Sharpe ratio, and maximum drawdown. Track these metrics in your account history or export data to analyze offline. Use the built-in risk tools like stop-loss orders, take-profit targets, and position sizing calculators to manage exposure per trade.

FBS Pricing: Which Plan Should You Choose?

FBS's account types are all free to open—you only pay through the spread (the difference between bid/ask prices) or commission on some accounts:

  • Demo Account ($0/month): Unlimited virtual money, real market prices, all features. Perfect for learning the platform with zero risk. Never expires on FBS.
  • Cent Account ($0/month, $0 minimum deposit): Live account with ultra-tight spreads, 0.01 lot minimum, and leverage up to 1:3000. Ideal for beginners testing real strategies with $1-$100. Spreads start from 0.1 pips on major pairs.
  • Standard Account ($0/month, $0 minimum deposit): Most popular account type. 0.1 lot minimum, spreads from 1.0 pip on major pairs, leverage up to 1:3000. Costs nothing to open; you only pay spreads when trading.
  • Zero Spread Account ($0/month, $0 minimum deposit): True zero spreads on major pairs (0.0 pips) but with 2.5 pip commission per round-turn. Best for high-volume scalpers and day traders who benefit from tight spreads despite commission.
  • ECN Account ($0/month, $0 minimum deposit): Electronic Communication Network access with 0.2-0.5 pip spreads, 1.0 pip commission, and leverage up to 1:500. Designed for institutional and professional traders seeking direct market access.

For beginners: Start with the Demo Account for 1-2 weeks, then open a Cent Account with $5-$20 to practice real trading without significant risk. This builds confidence and habit-forming discipline before graduating to the Standard Account.

For intermediate traders: Use the Standard Account. With spreads from 1.0 pip and high leverage, it suits swing traders and position traders with moderate position sizes ($100-$1,000+).

For advanced/professional traders: The Zero Spread or ECN Account makes sense if you trade frequently and volume justifies the commission structure. ECN is better if you need the most transparent pricing and access to institutional liquidity.

Pro Tips for Getting the Most Out of FBS

  • Use the Cent Account as a real-money testing ground. Many traders skip the demo, thinking it won't teach them psychology, then blow real accounts. Instead, fund a Cent Account with $10-$50. Because pip values are 1/100th of a Standard Account, your risk is real but contained. You'll learn position sizing, emotional management, and trade discipline without catastrophic losses.
  • Enable two-factor authentication immediately. FBS allows 2FA via email or Google Authenticator. Given that forex accounts with leverage can move quickly, protecting your account from unauthorized access is non-negotiable. This takes 2 minutes and prevents account takeovers.
  • Leverage the FBS educational hub before trading live. FBS offers free webinars, video courses, trading guides, and economic calendar training. Spend 2-3 hours in their education section learning FBS-specific features, MetaTrader basics, and risk management. This investment returns dividends in fewer mistakes and better understanding of your tools.
  • Set strict risk-per-trade limits in your account settings. Before you trade, decide your maximum loss per trade (e.g., $10 on a $500 account = 2% risk). Calculate your lot size accordingly and stick to it. FBS's risk calculator helps: input your account size, risk percentage, and the number of pips in your stop-loss, and it tells you the exact lot size. Write this number down and don't deviate.
  • Use the copy trading feature to learn, not as a complete substitute for learning. Copy a few successful traders while still paper trading to understand their entry/exit patterns. After 2-4 weeks, you'll recognize their style and can apply similar logic to your own trading. Copy trading is best as a learning tool for beginners, not a passive income strategy.
  • Test all EAs on the demo account and via backtesting before live deployment. FBS's MT4/MT5 backtester is powerful. Forward-test an EA on the demo for at least 500 trades over 2-4 weeks before adding it to your live account. This reduces the risk of poorly coded or curve-fitted strategies blowing up on live data.
  • Monitor the FBS economic calendar within the platform. FBS integrates an economic calendar showing high-impact news events and consensus forecasts. Set alerts for major releases (NFP, ECB decisions, Fed statements) relevant to your pairs, and avoid trading 30 minutes before/after these events if you're a swing trader. Day traders can profit from the volatility if they're prepared.

Common FBS Issues and How to Fix Them

Issue 1: High Leverage Leads to Rapid Account Drawdown

The Problem: FBS allows up to 1:3000 leverage, which is tempting. New traders often use 1:500 or higher, and a few bad trades wipe out their account in hours. Solution: Immediately lower your leverage in account settings to 1:100 or 1:200. Your account authority on FBS can change this anytime—it's not permanent. Use a leverage calculator to ensure your position size matches your risk tolerance. For a $500 account with 2% risk-per-trade, use 1:100 leverage. Once you prove consistent profitability for 3-6 months, you can gradually increase leverage.

Issue 2: Platform Connection Drops or Requotes During News Events

The Problem: Especially during high-volatility news events, MT4/MT5 may disconnect briefly, or your orders may face requotes (price rejections), preventing execution at your desired level. Solution: Ensure your internet connection is stable—use wired Ethernet instead of WiFi if possible. On MT4/MT5, set your slippage tolerance (usually 5-20 pips) to allow some price movement without rejection. Avoid trading the first 30 seconds of major economic releases; wait 1-2 minutes for volatility to settle. For critical trades during news, use a VPS (Virtual Private Server) to keep your connection to FBS's servers always active—many traders rent a $10-$20/month VPS specifically for this.

Issue 3: Spread Widening During Low Liquidity

The Problem: FBS's quoted spreads (e.g., 1.0 pip on Standard Account for EUR/USD) are tight during London and New York trading hours but can widen to 3-5 pips during Asian sessions or before major news. Solution: Check FBS's live spreads on the platform before trading. If spreads are abnormally wide, either switch pairs (usually minor pairs have worse spreads), reduce position size, or wait for better market hours. Consider the Zero Spread Account if you trade during these windows frequently; the commission structure may be better value despite paying per trade.

Issue 4: Difficulty Withdrawing Funds or Payment Method Issues

The Problem: New users sometimes struggle with withdrawals: payment methods are rejected, withdrawal requests are delayed, or funds take longer than expected. Solution: Verify your withdrawal method matches your deposit method (FBS requires this for most methods). If depositing via credit card, you must withdraw to the same card. For non-card methods, ensure your Skrill/Neteller account or bank transfer details are exact. Withdrawal times vary: cards take 3-5 business days, e-wallets 1-3 hours, bank transfers 2-7 business days depending on your bank. If a withdrawal fails, contact FBS support immediately via live chat (available 24/7) with your account details and transaction ID. They can troubleshoot payment processor issues or resend the withdrawal.

Is FBS Worth It? Our Verdict

FBS is worth using if you're a beginner or small-account trader who values flexibility and affordability. The $1 minimum deposit, zero account fees, free demo, and copy trading feature make it an excellent entry point for currency traders. The 3.9/5 rating reflects its strengths (accessibility, 24/7 support, multiple platforms) and weaknesses (offshore Belize regulation, extreme leverage temptation, and limited instrument diversity compared to brokers like Interactive Brokers or IG Group). If you're risk-conscious and disciplined about leverage, FBS's tight spreads and educational resources deliver solid value. However, if you need heavy regulatory protection or plan to trade stocks and bonds heavily, FBS vs Interactive Brokers shows better options. For copy trading specifically, FBS competes well with Pepperstone while maintaining a lower barrier to entry.
author: TradingToolsHub Editorial
